description: Trigger eval: assert which user phrases route to rails:ralph (this skill) versus ralph-skills:ralph (the PRD converter) versus neither.

should_route_here.input: setup ralph for this rails project
should_route_here.rationale: explicit bootstrap intent on a Rails app
bootstrap is this skill's exclusive verb

should_route_here.input: I want to run ralph autonomously on this rails project

should_route_here.rationale: worktree is this skill's signature side effect

should_route_elsewhere.rationale: explicit prd.json output, no setup mention

should_route_elsewhere.input: create a prd for a new feature
should_route_elsewhere.rationale: PRD generation, not Ralph at all
the user runs ./scripts/ralph/ralph.sh themselves; no skill handles this

should_route_elsewhere.input: fix a bug in active record

ambiguous_cases.rationale: by itself, could be either skill — leave to user disambiguation. The resolver should ideally surface both as candidates rather than silently pick one.

ambiguous_cases.input: I want to use ralph on this project
ambiguous_cases.rationale: 'use' is ambiguous between setup and convert; description should disambiguate via the Rails context cue
